moves.note: Best R/R 9.46, only enter if price…

plan: First priority is risk reduction: sell SOXL and SPAL immediately — both are leveraged, deeply negative, and together represent 8% of the book bleeding daily, and removing them alone cuts your worst-case drawdown meaningfully.

Trim SMH from 13% to 7% to reduce raw semiconductor concentration, which is currently doubling up with SOXL and CRWD in the same sector theme; the freed capital rebuilds a cash buffer rather than being immediately redeployed.

With the book cleaner, initiate PANW at 5% only within the scanner's entry range (351.34–351.69) with the hard stop at 350.62 — it carries the highest scanner conviction (9/12), a strong R of 6.01, and adds cybersecurity diversification away from pure chip/semi exposure.

Place ARM and TSM on conditional watch only — ARM has the best R/R on the board (9.46) but is a higher-priced volatile name, so size it at 4% and only act if price triggers the entry range; TSM at 3% adds quality semi exposure without leverage.
After all moves: total adds (12% gross) are funded by total trims/sells (14% freed), keeping net leverage flat-to-lower and leaving a small cash buffer to absorb intraday slippage or a gap-down open.
read: A consistent 2.5%/month (~34% annualized) is an aggressive target for any retail book, and this one carries real structural headwinds toward it right now: two leveraged losers (SOXL, SPAL) are actively dragging the average while concentration in a single volatile sector means one bad macro print on chips can erase several good months in days.
